title: nvm_read: out_len not clamped before truncate; stale zeros returned
description: nvm_read allocates data_len bytes (meta.len - offset), passes data_len to the C call, then does data.truncate(out_len as usize). If a misbehaving server returns out_len > data_len, Vec::truncate is a no-op (it only shrinks) so the returned Vec has data_len bytes — the trailing zeros from the allocation appear to be real server data. cert_read_trusted guards this correctly with .min(meta.len), but nvm_read and nvm_read_raw do not. Same flaw in nvm_read_raw: data.truncate(out_len as usize) with no upper-bound check.
